@@ -436,7 +436,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-qgk35.1","title":"[security] wa.send: extend analyze_content_risk to flag terminal-control bytes","description":"# Parent

ft-qgk35 asked whether wa.send's trust boundary should include "what
bytes flow," not just "which agent is authenticated." Investigation
found the policy engine **already has content-aware gating** — the
question collapses from "should we add a mechanism?" to "should we
extend the existing mechanism to cover a specific blind spot?"

# Mechanism already in place

`PolicyEngine::analyze_content_risk` (crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs:4792)
runs on every `ActionKind::SendText` with non-empty `command_text`.
It flags:

- **content.destructive_tokens** (40 pts): rm -rf, DROP TABLE, dd if=, …
- **content.sudo_elevation** (30 pts): sudo / doas / run0
- **content.multiline_complex** (15 pts): multi-line heredoc
- **content.pipe_chain** (10 pts): ≥ 2 pipes

And `PredicateKind::CommandPattern { patterns }`
(crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs:3296) lets operators write
regex-match rules on `command_text`.

So content-aware gating **exists**. The parent bead (ft-qgk35)'s
framing of "trust boundary is identity, not bytes" is incorrect —
the boundary already inspects bytes for a specific class of risks.

# The actual gap

`analyze_content_risk` is **shell-command aware** (rm -rf, sudo,
pipes) but not **terminal-control aware**. It does not flag:

- \x03 (Ctrl+C) — can kill another agent's foreground process
- \x04 (Ctrl+D) — can exit another agent's REPL/shell
- \x1a (Ctrl+Z) — can background another agent's work
- \x1b prefix (escape sequences) — can fake prompts, scramble
  the scrollback, clear the screen to mask evidence
- Bracketed-paste wrappers (\x1b[200~ ... \x1b[201~) — can be
  forged to make injected text read as human-pasted by agent
  detection heuristics

On the fleet of 200+ panes described in README.md, a compromised or
misconfigured agent that has scope to wa.send into a neighbor pane
today has unrestricted ability to type control bytes. The risk
surface is not hypothetical — agent A releasing its reservation on
pane B via a ghost Ctrl+C is a known class of coordination failure
in multi-agent terminal systems.

# Proposed tightening

Add a new risk factor to `analyze_content_risk`:

```rust
// Terminal-control bytes
let has_control_byte = text.bytes().any(|b|
    b == 0x03 || b == 0x04 || b == 0x1a  // Ctrl+C, Ctrl+D, Ctrl+Z
    || b == 0x1b                           // ESC (escape sequences)
);
if has_control_byte {
    self.add_factor(
        factors,
        "content.terminal_control_bytes",
        RiskCategory::Content,
        25,  // between multiline (15) and sudo (30)
        "Payload contains terminal-control bytes (Ctrl+C/D/Z or ESC)",
    );
}
```

Point value 25 chosen so default `risk_config.allow_max` still
permits typical interactive use (which should not contain control
bytes; agents that type `npm test` don't include \x03). Agents that
DO need Ctrl+C for long-running workflows trip the factor and
either:
  - pass through (if policy's allow_max accommodates 25), or
  - land in require-approval (if allow_max is below 25).

Either way the event is **visible in the audit trail** — which is
the actual ask from the parent bead.

# Alternative (higher-cost)

Introduce `PaneCapability::AcceptControlBytes` as a separate gate.
Richer but requires:
  - schema change to PaneCapabilities
  - migration for existing PaneState rows
  - config surface for operators to declare which panes accept
    control bytes

The risk-factor approach above is a ~10-line change with no schema
impact; the capability approach is ~150 lines and a migration. Pick
risk-factor unless a future bead explicitly wants per-pane
structural gating.

# Tests that should land with the fix

1. `analyze_content_risk_flags_ctrl_c`: "npm test\x03" triggers
   terminal_control_bytes factor.
2. `analyze_content_risk_plain_text_unchanged`: "npm test" does NOT
   trigger it (false-positive guard on printable ASCII).
3. `analyze_content_risk_flags_esc_prefix`: "\x1bc" (reset
   terminal) triggers.
4. `analyze_content_risk_flags_bracketed_paste_wrap`: the literal
   bytes `\x1b[200~...\x1b[201~` trigger (agent forging a paste).
5. `wa_send_dry_run_reports_control_byte_risk`: end-to-end from
   the MCP tool layer; dry-run with a Ctrl+C payload surfaces the
   factor in the injection preview.

# Disposition on parent

ft-qgk35 can be closed as `answered`: the mechanism exists; this
sub-bead tracks the specific extension. Keeping ft-qgk35 open risks
re-discovery of the same insight.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:02:15.998497Z","created_by":"content-trust-audit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:36:44.699744Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:36:44.698647Z","close_reason":"Verified in 010bac78","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","policy","security","wa-send"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-qgk35.1","depends_on_id":"ft-qgk35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:02:15.998497Z","created_by":"content-trust-audit","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-qhyfh","title":"[LOW][review] user_packs discovery skips sandbox_resolve_dir (ft-6mxfs follow-up)","description":"# Observation

From a review pass over 10 swarm commits in the last 2 hours (git log --since='2 hours ago'), specifically the ft-6mxfs canonicalization fix (0239fbd6).

## Context

ft-6mxfs closed a directory-boundary symlink escape in `.ft/patterns` discovery by routing through `sandbox_resolve_dir(&ws_dir, root_path)` before walking. The fix is correct and well-tested.

## The gap

patterns.rs:938 — when `config.user_packs_enabled = true`, the code takes a different branch:

```rust
if config.user_packs_enabled {
    if let Some(user_dir) = config.resolved_user_packs_dir() {
        let discovered = discover_packs_from_dir(&user_dir)?;
        ...
    }
}
```

This call does NOT route through `sandbox_resolve_dir`. The user-packs dir is either the user-configured `user_packs_dir` (tilde-expanded) or `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/patterns`.

## Threat-model delta

Unlike `.ft/patterns` (inside a cloned repo — attacker-controlled), `~/.config/wa/patterns/` is under the user's own control. So the escape window is narrower — the user would have to set up the symlink themselves, at which point they already have write access.

However:
- If the user shares dotfiles across machines and a symlink in their config dir points at something sensitive on one machine but not another, a stray `wa` invocation could read files outside the intended scope.
- The inner per-file `sandbox_resolve` (ft-fbee5) still provides a per-YAML defense, so this is defense-in-depth, not a primary vulnerability.

## Proposed fix

Call `sandbox_resolve_dir(&user_dir, &user_dir.parent().unwrap_or(&user_dir))` or pass an explicit `user_sandbox_root` so the canonicalized-inside-root invariant extends to the user-packs path. Keep the behavior that a missing directory is Ok(None) (already the common case).

## Review context

Found via multi-pass review after shipping ft-h9g0q follow-up (retry.rs PaneNotFound classification). Rating LOW because it requires the user to be the attacker-of-themselves.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:39:18.533748Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:48:37.894024Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:48:37.893825Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-qiba0","title":"[conformance] wa.mission_* / wa.tx_* (9 tools) lack schema+envelope golden roundtrip","description":"# Gap

Nine transactional tools in `tests/fixtures/mcp_manifest.json` have manifest-freeze coverage (schema gate, additionalProperties=false) but no envelope roundtrip conformance test:

* `wa.mission_state` — MissionRunState enum serialization, agent/action assignment maps
* `wa.mission_abort` — abort outcome envelope, idempotency on already-aborted missions
* `wa.mission_explain` — explain payload shape, trace graph
* `wa.mission_pause` / `wa.mission_resume` — lifecycle state-machine transitions
* `wa.tx_plan` — MissionTxContract shape, precondition list, classification field
* `wa.tx_run` — phase state transitions, compensating action record
* `wa.tx_show` — include_contract flag behaviour, chain verification data
* `wa.tx_rollback` — rollback outcome record

## Fix shape

Follow the same pattern as `mcp_conformance_rules_list_*` tests at mcp_conformance.rs:237-294 + the `agent_inventory_contract.json` golden shipped under ft-bs0ec (97ec6350). Recommended layout:

1. Fixture JSON per tool under `tests/fixtures/golden_robot_envelope/wa_{tool}.json` — canonical success envelope plus one denial/invalid-args envelope.
2. Test module `mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs` that calls each tool with deterministic seeded inputs, canonicalises the response (BTreeMap ordering, strip volatile timestamps), and golden-compares.
3. Reuse helpers: `assert_common_envelope_fields`, `assert_success_envelope_shape`, `assert_object_matches_documented_schema`, plus `UPDATE_GOLDEN=1` regen wiring from `agent_inventory_golden.rs`.

## Why this matters

Mission + Tx are the load-bearing transactional surface (precondition check, rollback, chain verification). A silent envelope drift here corrupts downstream client decision paths worse than a UI-level surface drift would — the transactional semantics are what callers depend on to unwind on failure. The mission_contract_golden.rs at line 80589845 pins MissionTxContract serde roundtrip but NOT the MCP envelope that wraps it.

## Scope guard

9 goldens + 9 tests + regen harness. ~500 LOC. No production changes unless drift is found during fixture capture — in which case a separate bug bead would be filed per drift.

## Explicitly out of scope

Core operator tools (wa.search/get_text/send/wait_for) — tracked under the separate core-tools conformance bead. Events/cass families (wa.events_*, wa.cass_*) — lower operator stakes; can wait for a later pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:50:18.619029Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T16:29:14.529199Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T16:29:14.528495Z","close_reason":"Already landed in d399a957, f20aedcf, and fa0f2d18: mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs + nine goldens cover wa.mission_* and wa.tx_* envelope roundtrips","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3634,"issue_id":"ft-qiba0","author":"jemanuel","text":"Pane 3 stale-open cleanup. The requested mission/tx envelope work is already landed in the checkout: d399a957 added crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs, the 9 golden_robot_envelope/wa_mission_*.json + wa_tx_*.json files are present, and parallel TOON harnesses now exist in wa_mission_toon_mcp_conformance.rs / wa_tx_toon_mcp_conformance.rs. Treating this bead as already shipped rather than duplicating the conformance surface outside the capture+storage+search slice.","created_at":"2026-04-24T16:28:29Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-qigup","title":"[deadlock-audit] notifications.rs:398: mute-store RwLock read guard is held across storage await","description":"Audit: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer concurrency scan of crates/frankenterm-core/src.

Real hazard: NotificationPipeline::handle_detection_with_cx acquires mute_store.read_with_cx(cx) at notifications.rs:398 and then awaits storage_guard.is_event_muted_with_cx(...) at lines 399-401 while the RwLock read guard remains live. The legacy handle_detection path has the same shape at lines 325-328. This holds the async RwLock across a storage await/DB operation, which can block any writer needing to swap or mutate the shared StorageHandle and makes cancellation/DB stalls propagate as lock starvation for writers. The nearby comment only says it avoids an exclusive lock; it still holds a read lock over an await.

Expected fix direction: avoid placing StorageHandle behind a runtime_async::RwLock for read-only mute checks, clone/extract the handle under the guard then drop before awaiting, or introduce a narrow StorageHandle clone/reference pattern. Add a regression proving a writer can acquire the mute_store lock while a mute check is pending/cancelled.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:38.572847Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:38.572847Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-qigup","title":"[deadlock-audit] notifications.rs:398: mute-store RwLock read guard is held across storage await","description":"Audit: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer concurrency scan of crates/frankenterm-core/src.

Real hazard: NotificationPipeline::handle_detection_with_cx acquires mute_store.read_with_cx(cx) at notifications.rs:398 and then awaits storage_guard.is_event_muted_with_cx(...) at lines 399-401 while the RwLock read guard remains live. The legacy handle_detection path has the same shape at lines 325-328. This holds the async RwLock across a storage await/DB operation, which can block any writer needing to swap or mutate the shared StorageHandle and makes cancellation/DB stalls propagate as lock starvation for writers. The nearby comment only says it avoids an exclusive lock; it still holds a read lock over an await.

Expected fix direction: avoid placing StorageHandle behind a runtime_async::RwLock for read-only mute checks, clone/extract the handle under the guard then drop before awaiting, or introduce a narrow StorageHandle clone/reference pattern. Add a regression proving a writer can acquire the mute_store lock while a mute check is pending/cancelled.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_2","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:38.572847Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:24:44.447226Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T05:24:44.446974Z","close_reason":"fixed mute-store read guard lifetime; rch verifier blocked by unrelated concurrent compile WIP in spsc_ring_buffer.rs/workflows/runner.rs and proptest_caut.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-qispe","title":"hyperlink shape hash depends on HashMap insertion order","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:16:28.784823Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:19:21.337317Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:19:21.337038Z","close_reason":"Already landed in fe515b29: sort hyperlink params before compute_shape_hash so equal links hash identically regardless of HashMap insertion order; regression exercised locally with --features std.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-qyw1z","title":"[review][CRITICAL] mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs WIP uses std::env::set_var, violates forbid(unsafe_code) workspace lint","description":"Uncommitted WIP expansion of crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs (pane 2, ft-4o5mb success-path harness) calls std::env::set_var and std::env::remove_var 6 times across FakeWezterm::install and EnvGuard::Drop.

Rust 2024 edition made std::env::set_var/remove_var unsafe fn. The workspace declares [workspace.lints.rust] unsafe_code = "forbid" (Cargo.toml), so this test WILL NOT COMPILE once committed — the harness currently builds only because the uncommitted diff has not been fed through cargo check --all-targets.

Evidence:
- git diff crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs: 6 set_var/remove_var call sites in FakeWezterm::install() and impl Drop for EnvGuard.
- Cargo.toml workspace lints: unsafe_code = "forbid".
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/lib.rs:44: #![forbid(unsafe_code)] (reinforced at crate level).
- MEMORY.md already flags this constraint: 'std::env::set_var is unsafe in Rust 2024 edition — use Command::env() instead'.

Fix options (pick one, no new beads needed):
1. Drop the FakeWezterm env-var approach. Instead, pass FT_WEZTERM_CLI/FT_FAKE_WEZTERM_STATE_DIR via a builder API that threads explicit config into the MCP server (no process-global mutation).
2. Spawn the MCP server in a subprocess with Command::env("FT_WEZTERM_CLI", ...) and talk to it over stdio — Command::env is safe and the workspace policy already endorses this pattern.
3. Convert the integration test to a #[test] that uses a hand-rolled test harness struct carrying the CLI path + state dir through Config, not env vars.

Tag: review. Severity: CRITICAL — blocks merge. Discovered by reviewer pane during 2026-04-23 audit round 2.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:01:22.783313Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:32:56.460683Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:32:56.460376Z","close_reason":"Replaced unsafe test env mutation with a process-local wezterm CLI override and updated MCP core-tool conformance coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-qzunb","title":"[HIGH] mux layout_arrangement + swap_layout JSON proptests fail on f64 ratio precision drift","description":"layout_arrangement_json_roundtrip and swap_layout_json_roundtrip in frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs currently fail at HEAD. arb_layout_arrangement generates LayoutArrangement::Split { ratio: 0.0f64..=1.0f64, ... } with recursive depth. When that ratio is serialized via serde_json and parsed back, some unrounded f64 values lose precision in the last decimal place (e.g. 0.10814827875804041 → 0.1081482787580404 and 0.9177432348398312 → 0.9177432348398313), so the roundtrip prop_assert_eq!(back, value) fails.

This is a blocker: the mux crate's cargo test currently shows 2/9 failing.

Fix direction: restrict the ratio generator to values that are exactly representable in IEEE 754 f64 with decimal notation (e.g. rounded to 1/2^16 fractions, or integer tenths/hundredths). Preferred: generate as integer 0..=10_000 and divide by 10_000.0 so every ratio is of the form n/10_000 and survives JSON's decimal representation. Keep the recursive depth/size parameters as-is.

Repro:
  CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
    CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane6-target \
    cargo test --manifest-path frankenterm/mux/Cargo.toml --test proptest_tab_wire_serde

Observed at HEAD (04dc8d76).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T17:59:44.814922Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:01:15.542629Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:01:15.542244Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 10309c8e: constrain arb_layout_arrangement's ratio to (0..=10_000).map(|n| n as f64 / 10_000) so every generated ratio is of the form n/10_000 and survives a serde_json decimal roundtrip bit-for-bit. All 10 tests in frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs now pass (was 7 pass / 2 fail). Granularity 0.0001 is more than adequate for split layout wire data.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codec","mux","proptest","regression","serde"]}
